metric_name: AWG core member count at study date
metric_value: 18 core members
notes: Up from 12 in March 1996

metric_name: AWG supporting member count at study date
metric_value: ~200 supporting members
notes: Up from 18 in March 1996; exponential growth cited

metric_value: 12 core members, 18 supporting
notes: Baseline for growth comparison

metric_name: ECF HL7 alpha software delivery status
metric_value: Alpha software distributed to core members August 1996; proof-of-concept demo October 1996
notes: All release dates met as promised; collaborative environment cited as key success factor

metric_value: Encapsulates HL7 standard in Enterprise Communicator software objects; eliminates need for app developers to parse raw HL7
notes: Key differentiator: standardization at implementation level, not just specification level

metric_value: 1997 target: incorporate DICOM imaging standard into ECF; simultaneous HL7+DICOM adds highly desirable EPR dimension
notes: Aberdeen recommends ISVs developing EPR systems track AWG DICOM activities

metric_value: 1997 target: incorporate IEEE MIB for medical device connectivity into ECF
notes: Aberdeen research: CIOs want better management of medical device-generated data

metric_value: Clinical Information Management Solutions — point-of-care for critical care, cardiology, surgery, perinatal, lab
notes: Combined clinical data measurements with computing, systems integration, and partner software

metric_value: Medical Diagnostic Equipment — ultrasound imaging, patient monitoring, cardiology, obstetrical systems
notes: HP long-term leader in point-of-care and back-end diagnostic equipment

metric_value: Computing Technologies & Professional Services — reliable scalable computing, open systems, Internet/intranet, HP OpenView, enterprise architecture consulting
notes: HP open systems emerged as leading platform for Internet/intranet in healthcare

metric_value: Transcends common interoperability interests; two highly influential groups consolidated vision and technical specs for standardization
notes: Aberdeen called this 'monumental step' rarely achieved in healthcare standardization history

metric_name: Aberdeen assessment of HP healthcare position
metric_value: HP in enviable position; care providers increasingly want total solution partner with individual products plus lead supplier for implementation and support
notes: HP Computer Systems Organization newly devoted to healthcare; strengthens overall position

metric_value: Industry support and actual standardization development efforts rarely aligned so succinctly in traditionally fragmented healthcare market
notes: AWG already coordinating actively with HL7 committee

metric_value: Total solutions provider: spearheads standards initiative + provides medical devices + offers enterprise computing + professional services
notes: Computer Systems Organization targeted healthcare as separate from Medical Products Group

metric_value: Standardization at implementation level via Enterprise Communicator components, not just specification level
notes: Prior standards failed because user-defined variables prevented plug-and-play; ECF solves implementation consistency

metric_value: Each core member commits a full-time senior architect for one year and agrees to use ECF in products
notes: Strong governance model; commercial product embedding commitment

metric_value: All 1996 release dates met; alpha software August 1996, proof-of-concept October 1996, as promised
notes: HP's collaborative environment cited as key success factor

metric_value: ECF commercially available by end-1997; fully embedded in leading healthcare IS supplier applications by 1998
notes: Based on AWG's stated commitments from charter core members

metric_value: HP will continue as systems-supplier-of-choice in healthcare; AWG will continue gaining momentum
notes: Aberdeen conclusion statement

metric_value: AWG Enterprise Communicators will bring application interoperability closer to reality than ever before
notes: Reiteration of prior Aberdeen prediction from 1996

metric_value: ECF adopted by HL7 for Version 3.0 standard; published in IEEE Transactions on IT in Biomedicine (1998); used in PACS/RIS interoperability
notes: Source: PubMed 9600413; academia.edu/32170701; doi.org/10.1117/12.274592

metric_value: HP Medical Products Group sold to Philips Electronics for ~$1.7B in 2001; ended HP's healthcare device leadership
notes: HP-Compaq merger focus led to divestiture of Medical Products Group; Philips Healthcare division formed

metric_value: Acquired by McKesson Corporation for $14B in 1999 (McKesson HBOC); subsequent accounting scandal
notes: 1997 core member of AWG; major healthcare IS consolidation event
